svg.font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif
svg.title: camerata architecture: how the MCP boundary works (target design)
svg.desc: The deterministic Rust orchestrator makes zero model calls. It prepares each session (a role rule file + an MCP config) and spawns agents behind the AgentDriver seam, model-agnostically — Opus as the strongest/orchestrator tier, and Sonnet/Haiku/open models as the tier-2/3 worker pool (open models via a claude-CLI to OpenRouter proxy). The strongest-tier orchestrator agent is the lead engineer; it partitions a story by repo/domain and requests delegation or fan-out through the gateway's orchestrator-only delegate/fan_out MCP tools. Camerata (not the model) then spawns domain/repo-scoped worker agents, which are leaf workers at depth 1 — they cannot re-delegate, the raw Task tool is disallowed, and built-in writes (Bash/Write/Edit) are disallowed. Every agent reads ungated but can only write through the gateway's gated_write tool. The gateway is the Layer-1 MCP boundary: it evaluates every call against the role's rules and denies or allows before anything executes, jailing each agent to its own worktree/repo scope; Camerata is the sole git committer and assembles one clean branch per repo. After development, one post-development-checks stage runs Layer 2 (mechanical), Layer 3 (the opt-in AI code reviewer), Layer 4 (origin CI), plus the orchestrator's cross-repo contract/integration gate; failures bounce back to the agent. The full enforcement model is in ENFORCEMENT_MODEL.md.
svg.text: camerata: how the MCP boundary works
an agent's only way to write — or to delegate / fan out — is a gated MCP tool; Layer 1 denies before anything executes
Camerata orchestrator — deterministic Rust · zero model calls
FleetCoordinator: prepares each session (role rules.json + gateway MCP config) · coordinates delegation + fan-out
SOLE git committer — agents have no git; the orchestrator assembles one clean branch per repo
AgentDriver seam (trait) · model-agnostic — the gate does not care which model
Tiered worker pool — Sonnet · Haiku · open
open models via claude-CLI → OpenRouter proxy (tiers 2 / 3)
the lead engineer: reasons · plans · partitions the story by repo/domain
built-in writes DISALLOWED: Bash · Write · Edit · Task
senior engineers: one per repo/domain (FE · BE · services · db · …)
LEAF · depth-1 — cannot re-delegate · no delegate / Task
tools: gated_write + Read · Glob · Grep · LS only
run in parallel, write-isolated per repo/domain
• gated_write → allow / deny before a byte hits disk — the ONLY write path
• delegate / fan_out → orchestrator-only; Camerata spawns the worker; depth-1; Task disallowed
• every agent jailed to its UoW worktree / repo scope — reads ungated, writes gated
evaluate_call(role rules, call) — the same gate for every model
Per-worker worktrees (write-isolated) — one per repo / domain
writes land ONLY when the gate allows · Camerata assembles → one clean branch per repo
after the work, the checks run here:
One MCP boundary (Layer 1) gates every write AND every delegation — reads are ungated; writes and sub-agent spawning are not.
Post-development checks (L2–L4 + the cross-repo contract gate) verify the produced code — full model in ENFORCEMENT_MODEL.md.
